title: Exceptional Cycles Arising from Nilpotent Representations
publisher: World Scientific Pub Co Pte Ltd
abstract: An exceptional cycle in a triangulated category with Serre functor is a generalization of a spherical object and a fractional Calabi-Yau object. We consider the bounded derived categories [Formula: see text] and [Formula: see text], and the cluster categories [Formula: see text] and [Formula: see text], where [Formula: see text] and [Formula: see text] are the hereditary abelian categories of nilpotent finite-dimensional representations of the quivers [Formula: see text] and [Formula: see text], respectively, with all arrows oriented in the same direction. There is a unique exceptional cycle in [Formula: see text] and in [Formula: see text], up to the equivalence relation [Formula: see text], and the actions of the auto-equivalences induced by these exceptional cycles are explicitly given. We point out that there are no exceptional cycles in [Formula: see text] or in [Formula: see text]. We also show that the possible length of exceptional cycles in a Calabi-Yau category [Formula: see text] is 1 or 2; if in addition [Formula: see text] is homotopy-like and [Formula: see text], then the possible length is only 1 and any exceptional 1-cycle is at the mouth.
